title: calliandra salvadorensis
intro: Calliandra salvadorensis, also known as the Pink Powderpuff tree, is a flowering plant native to Central America, characterized by its vibrant pink flowers and feathery foliage. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Calliandra salvadorensis include wound healing, reducing fever, and treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be taken internally to harness its medicinal properties.
